response.justification: The candidate is a capable polyglot developer with genuine geospatial interest and distributed-systems exposure, but the core requirements of this role — modern C++ or Rust for production routing pipelines — are not met. C++ experience is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; neither constitutes commercial routing or navigation work. The primary stack throughout the career is TypeScript and Node.js for web/SaaS products. The GIS experience, while authentic, targets NGO data collection rather than high-throughput tile generation or navigation guidance systems, and there is no evidence of working with routing tile formats, latency-critical map data pipelines, or traffic signal integration.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS background via GISCollective and Green Map, demonstrating sustained domain interest
Distributed-systems experience handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and low-latency optimisation at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated willingness to expand across stacks (DLang, Swift, C#, Java, Node) and a Linux-native development background
concerns: C++ experience is dated (2014–2015, < 1 year) and confined to desktop IDE tooling, not systems-level or geospatial pipeline work
No Rust experience; neither of the two required primary languages is in active use
GIS work is NGO-oriented data-collection tooling, not production routing tile generation, navigation guidance, or pipeline latency optimisation
